name: real-bug-report
description: A well-written bug report with reproduction steps, version info, and expected vs actual behavior

issue.title: Login returns 500 when email contains a plus sign
issue.body:
## Description

The login endpoint returns a 500 Internal Server Error when the user's email address contains a `+` character (e.g., `john+test@gmail.com`). The same email works fine during registration.

## Version

- App version: 2.4.1
- Node.js: 20.11.0
- OS: Ubuntu 22.04

## Steps to reproduce

1. Open the app at `http://localhost:3000`
2. Register a new account with email `testuser+bug@gmail.com` and password `Test1234!`
3. Registration succeeds and redirects to dashboard
4. Log out
5. Try to log in with the same email `testuser+bug@gmail.com` and password `Test1234!`
6. See 500 error

## Expected behavior

Login should succeed and redirect to the dashboard, just like it does for emails without a `+` sign.

## Actual behavior

The server returns a 500 error. The browser console shows:

```
POST http://localhost:3000/api/login 500 (Internal Server Error)
```

Server logs show:

```
Error: Invalid email format
    at validateEmail (src/utils/validation.ts:15:11)
    at AuthService.login (src/services/auth-service.ts:23:5)
```

## Additional context

I suspect the email validation regex doesn't account for the `+` character. The RFC 5321 spec allows it in the local part of an email address.
issue.author: careful-reporter
